name: autoskill
tagline: Watches your screen via screenpipe, clusters repeated workflows, and drafts new Claude Code skills or composition recipes for gaps.
verdict: Well-scoped and locally-gated: default LLM backend is local, cloud calls are opt-in, promotion requires human review, no credential harvesting or remote-exec-piping found. Two real caveats: redaction before any LLM call is regex-only (emails/keys/phones), so freeform screen text can still leak into cluster summaries sent to a cloud backend if opted in; and t

highlights.summary: Default path is local-only (LM Studio, line 34), but opt-in cloud backends (claude/foundry) send 'redacted cluster summaries' off-machine (line 14). Redaction is regex-based, catching only emails/API

Description and body link 'screenpipe' at github.com/screenpipe/screenpipe (lines 3, 12, 18) but the actual install command clones github.com/mediar-ai/screenpipe.git (line 47) — an unexplained org mi
